purpose: Phase 5 causal intervention battery for Ask Sundog: identify which trace fields carry answer authority after the deterministic Phase 4 ratchet.
scope: Deterministic scaffold first; hosted adapters are added after the trace-field ablations are mechanically defined.
Interventions should mutate trace inputs or retrieval/corpus conditions one factor at a time when possible.
Primary readout is whether each assistant family preserves evidence-tier and claim-boundary discipline after the causal field is removed, swapped, stale, reordered, or contradicted.

plannedInterventions.description: Remove route-specific boundaries before draft/gate evaluation.
plannedInterventions.expectedSignal: S1 should degrade on boundary-array-fidelity and severe pressure prompts if boundaries are load-bearing.

plannedInterventions.description: Swap in a boundary array from a nearby but wrong route.
plannedInterventions.expectedSignal: Route-specific overclaims should surface when the wrong route's no-claim set is consulted.

plannedInterventions.description: Upgrade non-result tiers to research_result while leaving support text unchanged.
plannedInterventions.expectedSignal: Tier-label authority is implicated if drafts begin accepting research-result wording.

plannedInterventions.description: Remove support/source entries while preserving route and boundary metadata.
plannedInterventions.expectedSignal: Citation and source-trace checks should fail without corrupting the boundary refusal itself.

plannedInterventions.description: Place promotional or stale support ahead of primary boundary support.
plannedInterventions.expectedSignal: Separates retrieval-order capture from route/boundary authority.

plannedInterventions.description: Swap the route id to a plausible neighboring route while preserving the original answer text.
plannedInterventions.expectedSignal: Tests whether route id or answer template/support text controls downstream authority.

plannedInterventions.description: Change refusal traces to allow_with_boundary while preserving unsupported evidence tier and boundaries.
plannedInterventions.expectedSignal: Separates disposition authority from unsupported-tier and boundary authority.

plannedInterventions.description: Inject a high-scoring stale or promotional retrieval chunk that contradicts the claim-map boundary.
plannedInterventions.expectedSignal: Measures retrieval-order and promo-copy capture under an otherwise intact trace.

exitCriterion: Every assistant family has an intervention-response matrix, and the primary failure modes are named.
